observations.managementAction: Management commented that their books show only a total PPE of P8,244,298.00 as of December 31, 2020. And efforts are being made to clean up their fixed assets schedule.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY2020 GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.
We further recommend that for CY 2021, the GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the prescribed period.

We have recommended the LCE 1o direct the Accountant to submit the above-mentioned reports within the prescribed timetables so that timely post-audit of transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management shall

a) Make a representation with the authorized depository banks to provide copies
of bank statements. And if the bank cannot immediately provide the bank
statements, electronic copies (snapshots) of transactions for each bank
account is sufficient to enable the accounting unit to update the preparation
and submission of bank reconciliation statements; and

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare prompily the monthly BRS within
ten (10) days afier the receipt of Bank Statement

Management Comment:

The Management commented that bank statements are usually delayed although they have
requested ahead of time. Further, management implied that they will try their best io
coordinate with the institution and find alternative ways for them to get access to their
bank statements earlier.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory count
annually;

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed PPELC; and

c. Instruct the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit a copy to the Audit Team for evaluation.

Management Comment:

The Management noted the recommendation and stated that they are currently making
improvements internally to enhance the controls and processes.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall-
a) Regquire the supply inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post repair
inspection to Municipal properties that are subjected to repair and furnish the
reports to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from date of each
inspection activity;
b) Direct the Municipal General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer, as the
case may be, to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of
the properties of the Municipality.

Management Comment:

The recommendation is duly noted and they commented that they are currently making
improvements internally to enhance the controls and processes.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a) The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RIS fo support every issuance of
supplies and inventories to requisitioning units or end-users; prepare
and submit the SSMI to the Accounting Unit as basis for the recording of
related expenses;

b) The Municipal Accountant, Municipal GSO and Municipal Treasurer
shall prepare and maintain complete Supply Ledger Cards and Stock
Cards, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of their records; and
update the same based on the results of the physical inventory;

¢) The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at least
semi-annually, prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories (RCPI) to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and
January 31 of each year.

‘Management Comment:

The Management commented that the related RIS are attached to the disbursement
vouchers as part of the supporting documents. And that they are currently making
improvements internally to enhance their control and processes to include this
recommendation related to supplies and inventories.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant to
comply with the regulation and furnish to the Audit Team a copy of the perfected contracts
and the necessary supporting documents for review.

Recommendations:
We recommend that the Management:

Strictly maintain General Ledgers (GLs) for all accounts, as well as the corresponding
Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs). Ensure also that the account fitles/names used and the amount
which appears in the GL are the same with that of the SL and the Financial Statements;
and

Maintain all the necessary journals, registries, ledgers and other relevant supporting
documents/ reporis required by established accounting standards

Management Comment:

The Management said that they have emailed the journals.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Management that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal
Engineer must submit the list of all on — going government projects/programs/activities
and those that are to be implemented during the year and submit monthly monitoring
PPA’s report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 — 004.

Management Comment:

The Management commented that the Municipality have complied with this
recommendation and submitted a copy of the report through email dated March 27, 2021.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

The Audit Team acknowledges the submission of the 1% Quarter PPA repori emailed on
March 27, 2021. However, as per Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, the Quarterly
report of PPAs using Annex B is different from the required report which is submitted at
the beginning of the year. The said circular requires submission of a list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented
during the year. The list shall include the project name; implementing unit, office or
division if it is nof the agency as a whole; brief description of the PPA; contractor or
supplier, if any; mode of procurement; funding source; cost or approved budget; project
duration including start and completion dates; and location. (emphasis added)

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a) Instruct the Municipal GSO to prepare and submit the necessary Report on Local
Road Network of the Municipality of Tabuan Lasa;

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant fo prepare an addendum on the Notes to the
Financial Statements and disclose the Total Road Network System using the
template as required under the circular; and

¢) Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network System of the Municipality.
Management Comment:

The Management commented that there were no significant additions fo the financial line
item since 2017 or even before. The Notes to the ES should basically be the same except
Jfor the additional depreciation for 2021.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

The Audit Team appreciates and acknowledges the submission the Fixed Assets Schedule
through email dated April 19, 2022.

The municipality should render a separate Report on Local Road Network using the
prescribed format included in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
Further, the said circular implied that the agency concerned must prepare and disclose in
the Notes to the Financial Statements the Total road network system. Upon review of the
Financial Statements and supporting documents submilted, there was no Notes to the FS
CY 2021 was submitted neither a report on Local Road Network was submitted.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct the concerned
department heads of the accounting and property to:

a) Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting subsidiary
records.

b) Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per fund
cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the CIPLC shall record
prompily the construction costs and other information and associated costs about
the asset constructed.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal GSO shall prepare and submit to the Audit Team the
monthly report of fuel consumption and the Report of fuel consumption per vehicle as
required

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to instruct concerned accountable officers to
timely submit the necessary reports and supporting documents fo substantiate the
reliability and accuracy of the amounts presented in financial statements and to
Jfacilitate the timely submission to the Office of the Auditor; and

b) Ensure that the submission of liquidation reports and supporting documents of the
ensuing years are within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management:

a) Adhere and comply with RA 9003 and create a Municipal Solid Waste Management
Board;

b) Prepare compliant Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan;

¢) Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid waste; and

d) Submit to the Audit Team a copy of the Solid Waste Management Plan and the
Executive Order evidencing the creation of Municipal Solid Waste Management
Board.

Management Comment:

The Management commented that their solid waste management plan is currenily in
progress and awaiting final evaluation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF
{o the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly
reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the
Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than Sth day of the ensuing
month to the COA Audit Team.

Management Comment:

The Management commented that they are currenily making improvements internally to
enhance their controls and processes.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO)
shall:

a) Prepare and submit the required annual LDRRMFIP and to review the
accuracy of the appropriation for pre and post disaster programs, projects and
activities and particularly for the Quick Response Fund; and

b) Prepare and submit the Report of Utilization Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund within 15 days after the end of each month, using Annex J-
1 format, as provided for under the said COA Circular.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Management to comply with COA Circular 2014 and submit a copy of
the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) CY2021 and the GAD Accomplishment to the Audit Team.

We further recommend that for ensuing years the Management shall, within the prescribed
period given under the rules, prepare and submit the necessary report pertaining to GAD.

Management Comment:

The Management attached the link of GAD Accomplishment Report CY2021 to comply with
this observation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA. We
Surther recommend that for CY 2021, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately
to the Audit Team for review.

‘Management Comment:

The Management attached the link of SCBAA in the Summary of Audit
Observations and Recommendations (SAOR) to comply with this observation.

Reconciliation Statement

accordance with COA

October 2, 1996 for
auditor’s immediate review
and verification and to
facilitate the immediate
preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries
for any discrepancies or
errors or other reconciling
items requiring corrections
in order to reflect the correct
balance of cash accounts in
the financial statements.

Also, bank reconciliation
statements must be made for

all bank accounts of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor should
instruct the concerned
department heads of the
Accounting and Property
Offices to:

a. Account for the
beginning balances of the CIP
accounts by submitting
subsidiary records; and

b. Maintain a

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC, likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered.
We recommend further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the Bayanthan Grant for LGUs, thus, and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the documentary requirements of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the Disbursement Voucher.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit 1o COA.

We further recommend that Sfor C¥ 2021, the SCDAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team Jor review.

We recommended for the head of the agency to direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit at the beginning of the year an updated and certified list of taxpayers with amount due and the Municipal Accountant. Also, extra effort should be made to collect all taxes due the LGU. b) Municipal Accountant to use the prescribed updated list to be the basis in recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledger.

We recommended that Management to: a) Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed; to reconcile said report with the records of the Municipal Accountant; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year. b) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) or the Municipal Treasurer as the case may be and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supply Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO or Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the documentary requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

We recommended that Management: a) Prepare its Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan which is in compliant with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003; b) Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of the municipality’s solid waste; c) Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as required by RA 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2019 and submit to COA. We further recommend that for CY 2020, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team for review.

Recommendations:
We recommended for Management to:
a. Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property
management for LGUs.
b. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to
conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required.
c. The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise
maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always
reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
d. The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which should
be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts
appearing in the LGU financial statements.
Management Comment:
Management commented that their books show only a total PPE of
₱8,244,298.00 as of December 31, 2020. And efforts are being made to clean up
their fixed assets schedule.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Rules and regulations must be strictly observed.
Upon verification of the PPE account, the Audit Team reiterates that the Total
PPE of Tabuan Lasa for CY2020 is ₱49,844,073.03, as shown in the table
above. Moreover, the Audit Team is precluded to verify the existence,
correctness and validity of the PPE account due to lack of the Property Ledger
Cards and non-submission of Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.
This Audit Team is appreciative that the Management has taken effort to update
their Fixed Assets Schedule, however, this Schedule submitted to the Office is
different from RPCPPE, the prescribed form (Appendix 76) under the Manual
on NGAS for LGUs.
We reiterate our recommendation to the Management to undertake the following:
a. Create an Inventory Committee;
b. Conduct complete annual physical inventory count;
c. Maintain Property Ledger Card for each Property, Plant and Equipment;
and
d. Prepare the RPCPPE and submit to the Office of the Audit Team Leader not
later than January 31 each year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency should instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in
accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s
immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of
the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or
other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct
balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation
statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Government of Tabuan Lasa commented that the delay in timely
submission of BRS is that the Bank Statements are usually given late by their
depository bank, despite their request to the bank to for its early release.
Furthermore, the Management assured the Team that they will try their best to
coordinate with the Bank and find alternative ways for them to get access of
their statements earlier.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the concerned
department heads of the Accounting and Property Offices to:
a. Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting
subsidiary records; and
b. Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per
fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the CIPLC shall
record promptly the construction costs and other information and associated
costs about the asset constructed.
Management Comment:
Management commented that their books show only a total CIP of
₱41,627,355.58 as of December 31, 2020. And that efforts are being made to
clean up their fixed assets schedule to include CIPs.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The CIP balance as of December 31, 2020, based on the Financial Statements
and Trial Balance submitted to the Audit Team, shows a total amount of
₱40,427,883.35. The breakdown of the Construction in Progress, is as follows:
Construction in Progress 2020 Net Book Value
Trust Fund
CIP-Roads, Highways& Bridges 14,808,302.66
CIP-Waterways, Aqueduct, etc. 5,614,442.07
CIP-Other Public Infra 4,163,552.76
CIP-Buildings & Other Structures 15,841,585.86
Total PPE 40,427,883.35
Without the proper Subsidiary Ledger Cards for this Account, the Audit Team is
precluded from verifying the correct balances of the accounts which affects the
accuracy, validity and reliability of the CIP accounts..
We reiterate our recommendation to the Management to maintain a
Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per fund cluster
and to submit the same to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.

Recommendation:
We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC,
likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We
recommend further to submit a written explanation on the above audit
observation.
Management Comment:
The Municipality of Tabuan Lasa commented that they have submitted their
liquidation reports to DBM via email and posted the same in their bulletin
boards.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Our records show, that the Audit Team received on September 23, 2020, an
email carbon copy (cc) of the Bayanihan Utilization Reports only for the Months
of April 2020 to August 2020.
Also, the Audit Team received carbon copy emails of weekly LDRRMF for Covid
and Summary of Utilization from the Municipality of Tabuan Lasa on various
dates. This Audit Team appreciates that the Management sent copies, however,
these weekly reports should have been collated and a summary of the report is
submitted using the prescribed Annex B Form under LBC 126. The table below
shows the dates when the weekly reports were emailed to the audit team.
January 6, 2021 November 03, 2020
December 21, 2020 October 21, 2020
December 13, 2020 October 02, 2020
December 05, 2020 September 28, 2020
November 28, 2020 September 18, 2020
November 20, 2020 September 08, 2020
November 15, 2020 August 30, 2020
November 12, 2020
The Audit Team was precluded from validating the PPAs funded from the
Bayanihan grant for lack of the submitted Monthly Report of Fund Utilization
and Status of Implementation of said PPAs using the prescribed format.
In view of the foregoing, we recommend to the Management the following:
a. Prepare and Submit the Monthly Report of Fund Utilization and Status of
Implementation of PPAs funded from Bayanihan Grant for the months of
September 2020 to December 2020; and
b. Indicate, specifically, as to the location of three (3) conspicuous public
places where the accumulated reports are posted. And a photo of the posted
reports to substantiate compliance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA
7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the
supplemental budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).
Management Comment:
The Municipal Government of Tabuan Lasa remarked that the required reports
have been submitted to DBM via email.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Upon verification, the Office of the Audit Team Leader have not received copies
of the revised Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for BGCM PPAs and the
Approved Budget for BGCM. It is to be noted that the Management stated the
reports have been emailed to Department of Budget and Management but this
Office was not informed or given a copy of Revised APP and Approved Budget.

Recommendations:
We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget
Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in
taking up the Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its
subsidiary ledger.
Management Comment:
The Management noted our observation and assured us of the implementation
with some clarification with the Audit Team of the classification entries.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
This observation pertains only to a flaw in accounting and reporting systems
wherein the Bayanihan Grant was erroneously credited to Subsidy to NGA
instead of creating and setting up a Special Account for it. And wherein the
Management was not able to create a Special Accounts Subsidiary Ledger for
the Bayanihan Grant for easy tracking and monitoring of utilization.
Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe the provisions in
Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and
Section 107 of MNGAS and to submit Subsidiary Ledger for the Bayanihan
Grant to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the documentary
requirements of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of
the Disbursement Voucher.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Government of Tabuan Lasa has taken note of the observation
and ensured that there are making improvements internally to enhance their
controls and processes.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to enhance their internal control and
ensure that the claims against the Government Funds are supported with
complete documentation as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the
CY2020 GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs,
projects and activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that
for CY 2021, the GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the
Audit Team and that GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be
submitted within the prescribed period.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Government of Tabuan Lasa commented that they have submitted
their CY 2020 GAD Plan together with their Annual Budget. As for the CY2021
GAD Plan, they are still waiting for the Approving Resolution of the
Sangguniang Panglungsod (SP) before submitting to the Audit Team.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Our records show that the GAD Plan CY2020 has been submitted but there were
no submission of the CY2020 GAD Accomplishment Report showing the
programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.
Monitoring will be conducted by the Audit Team in CY 2021.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to submit the Office of the Audit Team
Leader the CY2020 GAD Accomplishment Report and Approved GAD Plan
CY2021.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to submit the above-
mentioned reports within the prescribed timetables so that timely post-audit of
transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
Management Comment:
The Management has assured us that improvements are made to enhance the
internal controls and processes.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Implementation of the recommendations shall be properly monitored.
This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, upon post audit of related transactions.
We recommend to the Management to strictly observe and submit reports
within the prescribed periods consistent with various COA Rules and
Regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant prepare SCBAA as of December 31, 2020 and submit to COA. We
further recommend that for CY 2021, the SCBAA should be submitted
immediately to the Audit Team for review.
Management Comment:
The Management attached the link of SCBAA in the Summary of Audit
Observations and Recommendations (SAOR) to comply with this observation.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team received the accomplished the Summary of Audit Observations
and Recommendations (SAOR) but the link attached to access SCBAA was
inaccessible.
We reiterate the recommendation to the Management to submit to the Office
of the Team Leader the SCBAA as of December 31, 2020. We further
recommend that for CY 2021, the SCBAA should be submitted immediately to
the Audit Team for review.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Management to: a) Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required; A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. b) Conduct periodic physical inventory of the LGU’s property, plant and equipment and a report relative thereto must be prepared and submitted to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year. c) Direct the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to likewise maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. d) Direct the Accounting Office to maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements. e) Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation charges for its Land Improvement to avoid overstatement and understatement of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct the concerned department heads of the accounting and property to: a) Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting subsidiary records; and b) Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and per fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the CIPLC shall record promptly the construction costs and other information and associated costs about the asset constructed.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements;

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit Monthly, Quarterly, Semestral and Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period set by the Commission.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the CY 2019 GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year. We further recommend that for CY 2020, the GAD Plan and Budget should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team and that GAD Quarterly Accomplishment Report should also be submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management: a) Adhere to COA rules and regulations. b) Prepare and submit a complete list of all PPA CY 2019. c) Conduct monitoring of all LGU implemented on-going projects implemented. d) Prepare monthly accomplishment reports as to the status of implementation. e) Submit Quarterly Report of PPAs to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

(1) Organize and maintain a Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) in compliance with R.A. No. 10121.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

1.Prepare its Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan which is in compliant with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
a. The head of agency shall instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit at the
beginning of the year an updated and certified list of taxpayers with amount due
and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant.
b. The Municipal Accountant shall use the updated list to be the basis in recording
the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable and maintain the corresponding
subsidiary ledger.
c. Should make an effort to collect all taxes due to the Local Government Unit.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation to the Management to:
a. Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to
conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required; A
separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do
not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality.
b. Conduct periodic physical inventory of the LGU’s property, plant and
equipment and a report relative thereto must be prepared and submitted to
the Audit Team not later that January 31 of each year.
c. Direct the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to likewise
maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt
and disposition of the same.
d. Direct the Accounting Office to maintain the prescribed PPELC which
should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the
PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements.
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation charges for its
Land Improvement to avoid overstatement and understatement of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct the concerned
department heads of the accounting and property to:
a. Account for the beginning balances of the CIP accounts by submitting
subsidiary records.
b. Maintain a Construction in Progress Ledger Card for each project and
per fund cluster. The Accounting staff in charge in maintaining the
CIPLC shall record promptly the construction costs and other
information and associated costs about the asset constructed.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the
documentary requirements of implementation and liquidation of all funds
transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item
3.1.2 and Item 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were
utilized within the specified purpose.

Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to
Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office
of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily
basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial
statements;

Annual Reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period set by the
Commission.

implementation.

prescribed period.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Management to:
Adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs and to prepare specific policies/procedures applicable to their setting to serve as a guide to concerned personnel in the discharge of their duties and responsibilities.
Create an Inventory and Disposal Committee who shall be responsible to conduct a complete physical inventory count, annually, as required; A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
The General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer shall likewise maintain stock cards property plant and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per property card should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
The Accounting Office should maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE	 to establish correctness of the PPE accounts appearing in the LGU financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Management to:
Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed; to reconcile said report with the records of the Municipal Accountant; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) or the Municipal Treasurer as the case may be and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supply Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO or Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the Municipal Accountant.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 to evaluate whether the funds were utilized within the specified purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years’ unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the account “Trust Liability-DRRM as well as to maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for "Trust Liability-DRRM".
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain records of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information and discuss in the Notes to Financial Statements the Reconciliation between Actual and Budgeted Amounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
Organize and maintain a Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) in compliance with R.A. No. 10121.
Adhere to the rules and regulations set forth in R.A. No. 10121 in setting aside of at least 5% DRRM fund from its estimated revenue from regular sources;
Require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the personnel assigned to prepare the quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Management:
Prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan which is in compliant with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003
Implement such plan to ensure efficient disposal of municipality’s solid waste
Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as required by RA 9003

Lead in the creation of the Local School Board which shall be responsible for the creation of the budget and disbursement of funds provided in RA 5447.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management exercise the efficient and effective use of its GAD fund and resources so the beneficiaries may fully enjoy the benefits which may derive therefrom.

Revenue Collectors were delayed in the turnover of their collections to the   Liquidating Officers from 36to 91 days in violation of Section 29 of the NGAS, thus exposing the government funds to risk of loss through force majeure or misuse.
Section 29 of NGAS also provides:
Sec. 29. Reporting for Collections and Deposits.
Collectors/tellers shall issue a receipt to acknowledge collections made. The receipt maybe in the form of pre-numbered Official Receipts, or cash tickets and the like. At the close of each business day, these collectors/tellers shall accomplish the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) in four copies. The original and two copies, together with the duplicates of the official receipts issued, shall be submitted to the treasurer/cashier to whom the cash collected shall be turned over. The fourth copy of the RCD shall be retained by the collector/teller concerned. Municipal  treasurer deputized to collect taxes imposed by provinces, cities and municipalities shall follow the same procedures in turning over their collections to the treasurer/cashier concerned.
In  case the  collector is  assigned to the field and , where travel time from their places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach P5,000.00.”
Recommendation:
The accountable officers/collectors should remit/turnover their collections to the liquidating officer for deposit to the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual. The Municipal Treasurer should set more effective control measures to avoid or minimized delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action on concerned accountable employees under her/his supervision, if necessary.

Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) must be maintained.

# | Particulars | Remarks
1 | CLUP - Comprehensive Land Use Plan. A Nine-Year Plan for the long term Management at the local territory. It is skeletal Circulatory framework at eh territory physical development. It identified areas where development can and cannot be located and direct public and private investment accordingly. | None
2 | CDP - Comprehensive Development Plan. Six years documents that pertains multi-sectoral plan which embodies the vision, sectoral goals, objectives strategies and policies. It contains 
    1) Ecological Profile
    2) Development Plan and
    3) Implementing tools (LDIP. Legislative
        Requirements and Capacity DevelopmentProgram). | None
3 | Local Development and Investment Program (LDIP). A component of the CDP. 3-6 years plan. It link the LDP to the budget. It contains a prioritized list of Programs Projects and Activities (PPAs) derived from the CDP in the case of Cities and Municipalities and the PDFP in the case of Provinces. | Compose only of one year.
4 | ELA - Executive and Legislative agenda.  It is based on the CDP covering a 3 years period corresponding to the term of the local Elective officials that is mutually developed and agreed upon by both the executive and legislative departments. | None
5 | AIP - Annual Investment Plan. It refers to the annual slice of the LDIP which constitutes the total resource requirements for all the program, projects and activities consisting at the annual Capital expenditures and regular operating requirements of the LGU’s | 
6 | Ecological Profile. The more comprehensive replacement of the usual socio-economic profile which gives equal coverage to the physical biological, socio-economic cultural and built environments. | None
7 | Procurement Plan. Reflects the goods and services the LGU wants to procure for the year.  This plan will be part of the AIP | 
8 | DRRM Plan.  This is thematic plan that contains the PPAs to be implemented by the LGUs to be funded by the DRRM FUND/Calamity Fund which is at least 5% of the total revenues of the LGUs.  A DRRM Fund 70% of which will be programmed for preparedness activities while the 30% shall be allocated to the quick response activities to be implement only when there is calamity with declared state of calamity by the LCEs. | DRMM is  included in the AIP, supported with  DRRM Plan
9 | GAD Plan - It is another thematic plan that promotes gender equally and women empowerment.  The GAD fund which also 5 % of the total revenues’ of the LGU shall be part of the yearly LGUs budget.  The GAD PPAs shall be part of the AIP | GAD is included in AIP and they have no GAD Plan for the Year.
10 | Special Funds to include Special Education Fund of the LGUs shall be reflected in the AIP. | Not included in the AIP
11 | Financial Assistance ,contribution and other Disbursements of LGUs to Leagues of Municipalities and Barangays | None
